plainEnglish: Text must have enough contrast against its background: at least 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text (about 24px, or 19px bold). Low-contrast gray-on-white or light-on-light text is hard to read for people with low vision, aging eyes, or in bright sunlight. Logos and disabled controls are exempt.
whyItMatters: People with low vision, and the many users over 40 with reduced contrast sensitivity, can't read text whose contrast ratio falls below 4.5:1.
quickCheck: Use Chrome DevTools' color picker (it shows the contrast ratio with AA/AAA checkmarks) or run axe DevTools' color-contrast rule on the text in question.
commonMistakes: Light-gray placeholder or helper text (e.g., #999 on white) that falls below 4.5:1.
White text on a light brand color or over a busy image with no backing or overlay.
Assuming large headings are fine when they're actually below the 3:1 large-text threshold.
Hover or visited link colors that drop below the contrast minimum.

codeExamples.label: Body text with sufficient contrast
